title: The Spear as a Catalyst and a Stopper
display_title: The Spear as a Catalyst and a Stopper
wikitext:
[]: The Spear as a Catalyst and a Stopper             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:25 am So, I came up with this idea that seems a bit plausible... How about, the Spear of Longinus functions based on the side that is used: exactly, the 2 pointed-side acts as a "stopper" and the one-point side as a catalyst to whatever is going on... Wee see for example: -Adam impaled on Antarctica Which we know was done so by the Spear in the presence of the other Seed of Life -Lilith on the cross Part of this theory includes a sub-idea that Lilith also has the power to eliminate the life it created... Humans were next due to their unholy acts and/or simply because "they have reach the end of their evolutionary potential" and Nerv used the Spear to stop the natural upcoming process of their demise... On the other side (quite literally, btw) The only occasion on which we see the one-pointed-side of the Spear used is during 3I, when Eva 01 is transformed into the Tree of Life and later when the Eva spits the Spear and it takes the double 2-sided form... Also, when Arael and Eva 02 were destroyed, the Lances seemed to be used only as a physical weapon taking advantage of their AT field-piercing capabilities... On the point of "catalyst" I mean, we don't know for sure how long it would have taken 3I to end without it, but we know for sure that the Spear could stop it on it's tracks... I guess that's why Gendo was so desperate in getting rid of the Spear, so that we he started his own 3I, it could not be stopped, even if without the Spear as a catalyst it would have taken 3I to end as long as humans took to evolve from monkeys... What do you think?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:31 am That's rather impressive. It's an interesting theory, but I wouldn't begin to use it in my overall speculation about Evangelion without looking into it a bit more. I agree with you, though; the two-pointed side definitely seems to act as a stopper, and the one-pointed side as a catalyst. Q  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:37 am ^ That's a Q spoiler there!!! In a NGE discussion thread I'd suggest deleting the name of "the thing"! And my proposal only covers the NGE universe... We'll see later if it fits the RoE...

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:40 am Feh. It was in a spoiler tag. Anywho, edit made.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:55 am I think you're overthinking it. The Spear is a security device, full stop. It may pursue that functionality in different ways (piercing ATFs, immobilizing seeds, binding nascent seeds a la Unit 01, etc) but beyond that it doesn't seem to be required for or used in impact events. Lilith, for example, seemed to do just fine without it, and Adam triggered 2I despite the Spear's influence, not because of it. I think the Spear's a bit of a red herring in 3I, since Lilith doesn't seem to use it at all -- it's fully occupied in tying up Unit 01. If the Spear and Unit 01 weren't present I suspect 3I would have proceeded in similar fashion, albeit with someone else (probably Gendo) making the critical decisions. As such I'm extremely reluctant to assign any capabilities to the Spear that don't directly involve killing/controlling/binding stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:58 am Bagheera wrote: and Adam triggered 2I despite the Spear's influence, not because of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 24, 2013 11:21 am arael wrote: They removed the Spear in order to reduce Adam to the embryonic state... If I remember correctly, at the start of D&R we hear an OS voice saying something like "put the lance back"...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 11:27 am My understanding of the events on 2I were: Adam was found with the Spear (you remove the Spear, he awakes, Angels come to life, hell breaks loose) In order to obtain the S2 engine, Katsuragi required Adam active (of course, he was there for the S2 engine, whatever evil scheme SEELE had in store behind his back is something different [splitting Adams soul/body]) They remove the Spear and in the process of reducing Adam to Embryo and obtaining it's soul the explosion occurs (we actually never see anyone tang, maybe 2I was just a big explosion with underlying elements of philosophical and ethical perfidy)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 24, 2013 11:39 am arael wrote: Adam was found with the Spear (you remove the Spear, he awakes, Angels come to life, hell breaks loose)

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 11:42 am Bagheera wrote: No, they removed the Spear weeks before he revived. They sent it to the Dead Sea (presumably the real one) and everything

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 24, 2013 12:56 pm arael wrote: Wait... If that's so, how comes Gendo and Fuyutsuki are seen later in the ship with the Spear in Antarctica? Seems like and odd route, passing through Antarctica while going from the Dead Sea to Japan...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:19 pm Bagheera wrote: What do you mean? 2I happened 15 years before the events in the show. The Spear was removed from Adam, sent to the Dead Sea (to a research facility or something), returned to Antarctica right before 2I, and then stuck in limbo for 15 years before Gendo and Fuyu retrieved it. I've no idea why they left it there instead of getting it years earlier; seems like it might have been useful for whatever they were doing with Lilith, but you never can tell with these people.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:34 pm arael wrote: That's what I mean! It's silly to have an object they need on the other side of the world!

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Thu Jan 24, 2013 10:42 pm Bagheera wrote: After 2I it remained in Antarctica (or the waters that filled its void)

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jan 25, 2013 2:44 pm Bagheera wrote: I think you're overthinking it. The Spear is a security device, full stop.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 25, 2013 7:10 pm Clover wrote: It can also move things. Otherwise, someone should have stayed in orbit.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Feb 01, 2013 10:09 am Maybe that's referring to how the Spear leaves the Moon of its own accord during 3I, and the implication that the Spear is semi-sentient and has some control over its actions?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Feb 01, 2013 2:45 pm arael wrote: They removed the Spear in order to reduce Adam to the embryonic state... If I remember correctly, at the start of D&R we hear an OS voice saying something like "put the lance back"...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Feb 01, 2013 2:56 pm thewayneiac wrote: Actually, the voice is saying " pull back the lance", not "put back".
